items.title: January jobs report, McDonald's earnings, Fed: What to Watch
items.description: Market Domination Overtime Host Josh Lipton previews several of the biggest stories to come tomorrow, Wednesday, February 11, including the release of the delayed January jobs report; earnings results from companies like McDonald's (MCD), Cisco Systems (CSCO), and T-Mobile US (TMUS); and commentary from Federal Reserve Bank of Kansas City president Jeff Schmid. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Domination Overtime.

items.title: Stock market today: Dow, S&P 500 waver, Nasdaq slides after January jobs report smashes expectations
items.description: The January jobs report blew past expectations, bolstering the case for interest rates to stay on hold.

items.title: Stock market today: Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq surge as January jobs report exceeds expectations
items.description: The January jobs report blew past expectations, bolstering the case for interest rates to stay on hold.

items.title: Stock market today: Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq futures climb as January jobs report exceeds expectations
items.description: All eyes are on the January jobs report for a steer on rate cuts, as signs of weakness emerge in the labor market.

items.title: Stock market today: Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq futures climb as Wall Street braces for jobs report
items.description: Major gauges edge up ahead of Wednesday's jobs report, with low expectations for the health of the labour force.
